items.scenario: You are two weeks into a marketing internship at a local nonprofit. Your supervisor asked you to prepare a short presentation for Friday's team meeting, but she has not yet confirmed how long you will have to present.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Okafor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you have started preparing the presentation
Ask how much time you will have to present
Suggest a day to review your slides together before Friday
items.subject: Confirming my presentation slot for Friday's meeting

items.scenario: You recently began attending a weekly coding workshop hosted at your local community center. The workshop was moved from the main hall to a smaller room last week, and the projector there does not work properly.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Pruitt. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the projector problem in the smaller room
Ask whether the workshop can return to the main hall
Suggest testing the equipment before the next session
items.subject: Projector trouble after the coding workshop moved rooms

items.scenario: You signed up for a beginner pottery class at a local studio that begins next Tuesday. The confirmation email did not mention whether the studio provides aprons or clay tools for first-time students.
items.direction: Write an email to Mr. Whitcombe. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you are a first-time student in the class
Ask whether aprons and tools are provided
Mention that you would like to arrive early to complete paperwork
items.subject: Supplies for Tuesday's beginner pottery class

items.scenario: Your academic advisor recommended a popular seminar that requires instructor permission to join. The course website lists a waitlist of over thirty names but does not explain how students are selected off it.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Liang. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain your interest in the seminar
Ask how students are chosen from the waitlist
Mention relevant coursework you have completed in the subject area
items.subject: Getting off the seminar waitlist
